title: Ensure Closing Braces and Keywords 'else', 'catch', 'finally' are on Different Lines
description: This rule ensures that a closing curly brace and the subsequent 'else', 'catch' or 'finally' keyword are placed on two different lines. This contributes to a shared coding convention and boosts team collaboration efficiency.
recommendation: Always put closing braces and the subsequent 'else', 'catch', 'finally' keywords on separate lines. This improves readability and aligns with common Java coding conventions.
impact: Improves code readability and maintainability. Not following this rule might lead to code misunderstanding and potential bugs.

checks.message: Closing brace and subsequent 'else', 'catch', 'finally' keywords should be on different lines
